mnemonics: Master Backward Induction, Make Rational Decisions Easily
keyPoints: A finite horizon game is one where the number of stages is finite.
The key to finding sub-game perfect equilibria is to use backward induction.
At each stage, players look at the stage after them and decide accordingly.
The backward induction process continues until the last stage is reached.
The equilibrium is achieved when no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy.
The sub-game perfect equilibrium is the Nash equilibrium of the entire game.
Backward induction can be used to solve zero-sum games and non-zero-sum games.
In zero-sum games, the sum of all payoffs is zero. In non-zero-sum games, the sum is not zero.
